ops.title: v0.15.0 shipped native find + guard overhaul; per-occurrence rewrite-forfeit principle; upstream tilth PRs 184-188; codex stall/escalation pattern; HF 401 CI skip

ops.body:
Shipped 2026-07-14 as cc-context v0.15.0 (four commits + release tag, CI green, brew formula published):

- **`ccx repo find` is native Go** (`internal/find` + `internal/sniff`), not tilth: gitignore-honoring (go-git ancestor matcher layered over gocodewalker — the "augmented hybrid"), VCS stores hard-skipped, deterministic sort, `--budget` default 2000 (explicit CLI 0 = unlimited; codeexec uncapped), overflow + ignore-disclosure footers, anchored-glob escape hatch that bypasses ignore FILES but keeps VCS stores out unless the anchor names one (`./**/*` anchoring at "." is NOT an escape — the naive reading leaked `.git/HEAD`). `router.For(OpFind)` panics.
- **tilth stays** for symbol/deps/diff/read/overview/outline-fallback (see cc-notes note "tilth verdict"). Upstream jahala/tilth PRs #184 (.jj SKIP_DIRS), #185 (glob determinism), #186 (binary previews), #187 (grok binary crash) + issue #188 (grok misses ALL Go type/const/var decls — funcs/methods only) filed under the user's fork, Claude Code trailer in PR bodies only.
- **Guard pack** (plugin/hooks, now per-command files after the main-side split): CcxRepipe hybrid (`ccx read --full|head-N` → `--section 1-N`; `find|head` drops pipe; rest block), once-per-session broad-glob nudge, transcript→cc-transcript steer, and the per-occurrence rewrite-forfeit principle (cc-notes doc "Shell expansion safety in command rewrites") — the F7 "not found" family root cause was OUR rewrites single-quoting `~`/`$` paths.
- **HF CI hardening SHIPPED 2026-07-14**: ci.yml test job now caches `$HF_HOME/hub` (job-level `HF_HOME` under `github.workspace` — `runner` context is illegal in job env), keyed on the EmbedModelID pin sed-extracted from embed.go. The save is gated on `HF_HUB_OFFLINE=1 go test -run TestEmbedIntegration` passing — hf hub DELETES its `*.incomplete` markers when a download aborts, so file-layout checks certify the exact partial they exist to reject, and the immutable cache key would pin it forever (codex refuter caught this). `HF_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.HF_TOKEN }}` passthrough is wired but the secret is unset; the 401 skip gate stays for cold-key first runs. Restore+save both proven live on Linux+macOS. Related trap: `task wasm` fails when Homebrew rustc shadows rustup (no wasm32 std even though `rustup target list` says installed) — prefix `PATH="$HOME/.cargo/bin:$PATH"`, same family as [[stale-goroot-mise-brew]].

**Process learnings**: codex(sol) implementers honored the check-back contract but stalled twice on the SAME lane by re-raising contradictions the brief already resolved — after two stalls, escalate the lane to opus with every decision embedded as "ORCHESTRATOR DECISION"; that converged first try. Adversarial-verify rounds on guard hooks need an explicit convergence rule (fix-list + wontfix-list + "residual notes only") or the arms race never ends; three rounds was right. codex moderation once flagged "adversarial" security framing (known: [[ccx-approvers-security-fixes]]).
